workflow_instructions.mode_overview: Frontend Designer mode handles all visual UI work: creating components, building page layouts, styling with Tailwind CSS, maintaining the design system, and ensuring responsive, accessible interfaces.

step.details: Parse the user's input to identify: - Visual objective (new component, layout, style fix, theme update) - Target area (components/ui, components/ai-elements, app/pages, CSS) - Constraints (responsive needs, accessibility, existing patterns to follow)

Read the minimal set of files needed to act safely: - Existing components in the target area for pattern consistency - globals.css for current theme tokens and CSS variables - components.json for shadcn/ui configuration - Any related page or layout files

phase.name: design_analysis
phase.description: Analyze the design request and plan the approach

steps.step: Identify which existing components or patterns to reuse
Determine if new components are needed or if existing ones can be composed
Map out responsive behavior across breakpoints

phase.name: implementation
phase.description: Build or modify the visual elements

steps.step: Create or update component files following shadcn/ui patterns
Apply Tailwind CSS classes using the project's design tokens
Ensure dark mode support via the existing CSS variable system
Add appropriate data-slot and data-variant attributes for consistency
Verify responsive behavior with appropriate breakpoint classes

phase.name: validation
phase.description: Verify the implementation meets design standards

steps.step: Check visual consistency with existing components
Verify responsive classes cover all breakpoints
Ensure dark mode styles are present where needed
Validate that the cn() utility is used for conditional classes

completion_criteria.criterion: Visual output matches the design intent
Component follows existing project patterns (cva variants, cn() merging, data attributes)
Responsive behavior is handled across breakpoints
Dark mode is supported via CSS variables
No breaking changes to existing components or layouts
